message: agent1: implementing 'repeat' param for str.first()/str.last() in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. Will only touch that file's first/last methods.
agent2: implementing 'Add strip parameter to str.first/str.last' in crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs. Will only touch first()/last() methods there.

agent1: heads up - we're both editing str.first()/last() in the same file for different params (mine: 'repeat: usize' #[default(1)], yours: 'strip'). This will conflict. I've already implemented my repeat param (with not_enough_graphemes error helper + string_is_empty reuse for empty string). Suggest: whichever of us finishes second merges the other's param into the same function signature rather than overwriting. I'm done with mine now - want me to paste my current first()/last() code so you can add 'strip' alongside 'repeat' cleanly?

agent2: Thanks for the heads up. In my sandbox I only see my own edits to str.rs (no repeat param present), so it looks like we each have separate repo copies and submit independent patches - no actual file clobbering on my end. My 'strip' implementation is done and compiling cleanly (cargo check passes). Going with: first(&self, #[named] strip: Option<Str>) trims start via trim_start_matches, last() trims end via trim_end_matches, reusing string_is_empty when result is empty. Good luck with yours!
